exemplar.title: To what extent does social media content and advertisements lead to future teenagers gambling addiction problems.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does social media content and advertisements lead to future teenagers gambling addiction problems.

strengths: The inquiry question is succinct and clearly frames the investigation into social media algorithms and teenage gambling addiction.
Identification of a relevant real-world example (sports-gambling content on Twitter) shows appropriate focus.
weaknesses: Connections to course concepts (Health, Ethics, Systems, Algorithms) remain superficial and lack depth.
The explanation of how Twitter algorithms specifically influence teenage gambling promotion is underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the descriptor for Band 2 by stating a clear inquiry question and linking to a specific real-world example with course concepts in bullet-point form. However, it lacks the detailed, coherent rationale and depth required for a Band 3 (thorough) explanation, so a score of 2 is warranted.

strengths: Three sources are clearly summarised with discussion of their relevance to teenage gambling via social-media algorithms.
Use of headings (âCriterion B: Claims and perspectivesâ) enhances structural clarity.
Acknowledgement of study limitations and research methods in one source demonstrates engagement with credibility issues.
weaknesses: Discussion remains largely descriptive with limited critique of authorsâ biases and methodologies.
Insufficient evaluation of contrasting viewpoints and deeper justification of each sourceâs usefulness.
Citation formatting is inconsistent and narrative summaries lack explicit critical analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student presents a partial discussion of claims and perspectives, which aligns with lower Band 3. There is a clear attempt to justify source usefulness, but it is not fully developed. Elevating the score to 4 reflects movement toward the mid-range of Band 3â4, recognizing structural clarity and engagement with limitations while noting the need for deeper critical evaluation.

strengths: Identifies key issues such as mental-health impacts, government regulation, and corporate power dynamics.
Attempts to link analysis back to the inquiry focus.
weaknesses: Analysis is brief and largely descriptive, lacking sustained evaluation of impacts on people and communities.
Claims are not well-supported by evidence or theoretical frameworks.
Limited development of implications for stakeholders or broader contexts.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis shows some limited relevance to the inquiry focus but is not sustained or well-supported, placing it at the upper end of Band 1â2. A score of 2 reflects adequate recognition of impacts but insufficient depth for higher bands.

weaknesses: No distinct conclusion section is presented.
There is no synthesis of findings or discussion of emerging trends and future developments.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work fails to meet even the limited descriptor for Band 1â2 (no further insight or synthesis), warranting a score of 0.

strengths: Clear use of headings and subheadings provides a recognizable structure.
The âConceptsâ section effectively organizes theoretical frameworks for reader navigation.
weaknesses: Formatting glitches (errant symbols) and informal phrasing disrupt coherence.
Citation format is inconsistent and bibliographic entries are fragmented.
Transitions between sections are sometimes disjointed, affecting flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is organized and largely coherent, with effective use of structure, meeting the Band 3 descriptor. Despite formatting and citation weaknesses, its overall communication quality merits the maximum 3 marks for this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning: Analysis is confined to short statements about potential mental-health effects, government regulation and corporate power.  These remarks are broad, lack evidence, and are not developed into sustained evaluation of impacts on people or communities.  Consequently the work shows only limited, mainly descriptive analysis consistent with Band 1-2; given the minimal depth, the lower mark of 1 is warranted.

Headings, sub-headings and citation lists provide a recognizable structure, and the reader can broadly follow the argument.  However, formatting glitches (math-mode symbols, errant characters) and some disjointed transitions affect coherence.  No multimedia is referenced beyond hyperlinks.  Overall organisation is adequate but not consistently effective, matching Band 2 (2 marks).

The student states a clear inquiry question and identifies a real-world example (sports-gambling content on Twitter).  Basic links are sketched to course concepts such as Health, Politics, Power, Values & Ethics, Systems, Algorithms and Media; however these links are largely bullet-point notes with limited depth or evidence.  The explanation therefore goes beyond a mere mention but lacks the detailed, coherent rationale required for a âthoroughâ descriptor.  This aligns most closely with Band 2 (partial explanation).

Three separate sources are summarised, each accompanied by some discussion of the authorsâ positions and how the material relates to teenage gambling via social-media algorithms.  The student comments on relevance (e.g., youth exposure, need for regulation, cultural shifts) which provides partial justification of usefulness.  Nonetheless the treatment remains largely descriptiveâcredibility, limitations or contrasting viewpoints are only briefly touched upon.  This places the work in the lower end of Band 3-4, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

No distinct conclusion section is presented.  There is therefore no synthesis of findings or discussion of emerging trends/future developments.  The work fails to meet the minimum descriptor for Band 1-2 and must be awarded 0.

annotations.comment: The inquiry question is succinct and focused, clearly framing the investigation into social media algorithms and teenage gambling addiction.

The real-world example is relevant but the connection to course concepts remains superficial; further explanation of how Twitter algorithms specifically influence gambling promotion would strengthen the focus.
Expand the Health context by referencing specific studies or statistics on gambling-related anxiety among teenagers to support the claim with concrete evidence.
Consider discussing particular regulatory frameworks or bills to illustrate how government intervention can shape algorithmic content distribution.
The clear use of the âConceptsâ section organizes the theoretical framework effectively, aiding reader navigation.
The description of Systems is vague; specifying how algorithmic filtering mechanisms function would deepen the analysis of content reach.
Integrate theoretical frameworks on power relations or reference studies demonstrating corporate influence on algorithm design to substantiate this claim.
The ethical discussion emphasizes company revenue but overlooks the ethical responsibility toward vulnerable groups; consider addressing stakeholder ethics.
The stray mathematical symbol appears as an OCR artifact and disrupts the flow; remove or clarify its relevance to improve readability.
The âCriterion B: Claims and perspectivesâ heading clearly delineates the transition to source evaluation, enhancing structural clarity.
Embed the URL within a proper citation format and briefly justify why this source is pertinent to your inquiry.
This summary is descriptive; evaluate the authorsâ perspectives and discuss source limitations to develop a critical discussion of its usefulness.
The description is detailed but overly narrative; condense the text and integrate explicit commentary on the authorsâ claims and standpoint.
The clear source title distinguishes the origin of your information, aiding in tracking claims across your research.
Convert this raw URL into a full bibliographic entry and check link accessibility to ensure academic rigor and reliability.
While comprehensive, this review lacks critique of methodology; include evaluation of sample size and generalizability to strengthen source analysis.
Identify the authorâs stance and any potential biases to provide a balanced perspective on the sourceâs contribution.
The summary acknowledges study limitations and cross-country sample, demonstrating engagement with research methodsâthis supports credible evaluation.
Connect these findings back to your inquiry question by explaining how identity bubbles on social media may exacerbate teenage gambling behavior.
Informal phrasing (âin a fairly major wayâ) reduces academic tone; revise for formal language to maintain scholarly style.
Blog posts may lack peer review; critically evaluate the authorâs credentials and consider more scholarly sources to support algorithm analysis.
Standardize citation format and ensure each entry includes full publication details to enhance academic communication.
How do these listed works connect to your in-text citations? Ensure every reference is cited within the analysis for coherence.
The paper lacks a dedicated conclusion synthesizing your analysis; add a conclusion section that discusses emerging trends and future implications to fulfill Criterion D.
The continuation of the citation lacks context; confirm that page breaks do not fragment entries and maintain consistency in the bibliography.
